content.rendered:
When Bruce’s mom complained that he always sang about his father but never about her, Bruce replied by covering “A Woman’s Got the Power” by the great Philly band The A’s during a Meadowlands appearance early on the Born in the U.S.A. Tour. (This was before Bruce wrote “The Wish.”)

On-stage, Bruce credited the song to Clarence Clemons and the Red Bank Rockers, and brought RBR vocalist J.T. Bowen on stage to sing it with him. And yes, Clarence and the Red Bank Rockers did release their own version a year prior in 1983.

…but as a Philly child of the 80s, hometown pride compels to me to credit local band The A’s for the original, released in 1981. Their version will always be my favorite.

A Woman’s Got the Power
First performed: August 9, 1984 (East Rutherford, NJ)
Last performed: October 22, 2011 (Asbury Park, NJ)

© March 1, 2018

excerpt.rendered: Bruce salutes his mom with this song by local Philly band The A’s.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me/RotD: Clarence Clemons and The Red Bank Rockers: Summer on Signal Hill
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A lost original Springsteen song survives as a Red Bank Rockers instrumental--but whatever happened to the lyrics?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice/Cover Me, Clarence Clemons: Savin’ Up
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Double-entry today: "Savin' Up" is a Bruce Springsteen original composition, recorded by Clarence Clemons and the Red Bank Rockers, but performed live by Bruce on many occasions.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Rockin’ Pneumonia And The Boogie Woogie Flu
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Three times only: Bruce teamed up with The Red Bank Rockers and The Tangiers Blues Band on Huey Smith's barroom classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 3
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Today in Springsteen history: Bruce releases two new videos, a star-studded holiday show in Asbury Park features the return of some notable set list prodigals, and an epic 20-minute "Kitty's Back." More highlights inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 17
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce kicks off a multi-year tradition of Asbury Park Holiday shows. Plus: a Tonight Show appearance, last performances of several songs for years to come, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Do You Want to Dance
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Listen to Bruce Springsteen join Rhode Island rockers Beaver Brown in a joyful cover of Bobby Freeman’s “Do You Want to Dance” in a surprise appearance in the summer of 1982.
